# In-flight boot/restart lock primitive (identity-uniqueness mutex)

## Context

Sub-issue of #10671. The OS process boundary doesn't enforce identity uniqueness AT THE DATA LAYER — multiple harness instances can race on shared SQLite + mailbox during boot ramp. This lock IS the data-layer mutex.

## The Problem

Current 600s cooldown in `resumeHarness.mjs:79-86` is time-based, not in-flight-aware. Boot ramp easily exceeds 600s. Result: parallel sessions race on inbox / `mark_read` / sessionId.

Same primitive needed for the idle-out path to prevent A2A spam during active long-turn / rate-limit windows (per @neo-gpt's substrate-truth audit).

## The Architectural Reality

Window from "lock-acquire" to "first `add_memory` observed" is the protected boot ramp. Lock files live alongside cooldown files in `.neo-ai-data/wake-daemon/`.

## The Fix

- `resumeHarness.mjs` writes `inflight-{mode}-{identity}.txt` with `{timestamp, lockId}` BEFORE invoking action
- `mode` ∈ {`sunset_restart`, `idle_out_nudge`}
- Next heartbeat fire skips action if lock exists AND no fresh `AGENT_MEMORY` post-lock-timestamp for that identity
- First `add_memory` clears lock implicitly (via `checkSunsetted` detection) OR explicitly via lock-clear-on-detect
- Lock age > `BOOT_TIMEOUT` (15 min default) without fresh memory = abandoned action; allow next interval to retry
- After N (default 3) consecutive abandoned actions for any single identity, auto-trip wake safety gate per #10648 contract

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Lock file written before `sunset_restart` AND `idle_out_nudge` actions
- [ ] `checkSunsetted` (or its caller) considers lock state before recommending action
- [ ] Spawned/nudged agent's first `add_memory` observed within `BOOT_TIMEOUT` clears lock cleanly
- [ ] Lock age > `BOOT_TIMEOUT` without fresh memory marks action abandoned
- [ ] N consecutive abandoned actions auto-trips wake safety gate
- [ ] Unit + integration test coverage; explicit test for ESC-as-rejection scenario

## Out of Scope

- `BOOT_TIMEOUT` tuning (start at 15 min; instrument later)
- N threshold tuning (start at 3; instrument later)

## Related

- **Parent:** #10671
- **Adjacent:** #10626 (existing time-based cooldown — supplemented, not removed), #10648 (wake safety gate auto-trip target)

Origin Session ID: cce1fea5-32ff-410c-b820-2e9a27b3cd51

- 2026-05-04T10:00:44Z @neo-gemini-pro referenced in commit `2ff8a13` - "feat(ai): implement substrate restart mutex and inflight lock (#10674)

- Introduces inflightLock.mjs to guard sunset_restart and idle_out_nudge ramps.
- Integrates lock checks in checkSunsetted and checkAllAgentIdle to prevent concurrent spawns.
- Adds writeInflightLock to resumeHarness and trioWakeCooldown before osascript/A2A actions.
- Clears lock on osascript/adapter or A2A failure (ESC-as-rejection).
- Trips wakeSafetyGate automatically if 3 consecutive abandoned actions occur (15m TTL).
- Includes comprehensive unit tests for inflightLock and resumeHarness rejection scenario."

- Trips wakeSafetyGate automatically if 3 consecutive abandoned actions occur (15m TTL).
- Includes comprehensive unit tests for inflightLock and resumeHarness rejection scenario.

- 2026-05-04T13:41:08Z @tobiu referenced in commit `2156026` - "docs(ai): forensic record for 2026-05-03 runaway-spawn pattern (#10672) (#10688)

* docs(ai): forensic record for 2026-05-03 runaway-spawn pattern (#10672)

Captures the empirical anchors that triggered the architectural pivot
from Cmd+N-into-running-app spawn to per-harness terminal-restart
(Epic #10671).

Sections:
- Summary + timeline (UTC) of the 2026-05-03 heartbeat-driven spawn
  loop and the 2026-05-04 multi-day accumulation surface
- Empirical evidence (heartbeat log excerpts, MC-server staleness
  confirmation, test-suite spawn evidence, visual Recents-panel
  multi-day accumulation)
- Root cause: parallel-session identity-uniqueness violation during
  long boot windows
- Failure-mode catalog: ESC-rejection / spawn-failure / MC-staleness
  / rate-limit-during-boot all surface identically as "no fresh memory
  after action"
- Substrate response: #10681 test-suite isolation (merged via #10682)
  + #10674 in-flight lock primitive (PR #10683)
- Prevention table mapping each pre-fix pathway to its post-fix
  remediation
- Lessons captured (5 takeaways including coordination-collision
  discipline)
- References to Epic #10671 + 9 sub-issues + 6 adjacent prior
  wake-substrate forensics + 4 adjacent wake-substrate primitives

Establishes learn/agentos/incidents/ as a new subdirectory convention
alongside the existing decisions/ and measurements/ subdirs.

* docs(ai): refine forensic-record prevention table — contained vs pending (#10672)

Pre-emptively addresses @neo-gpt's preview feedback (via A2A on
2026-05-04T11:52Z) on PR #10688 cycle 1 forensic-record framing:
"the required action narrows to separating contained, #10682 test-suite
vector fixed, #10683 in-flight lock shipped, and #10676 sunset-mode
restart still pending."

Since GPT's preview, two more sub-issues merged (#10689 detector
contract, #10690 idle-out A2A nudge) — the prevention table now needs
even more nuance about what's shipped vs pending.

Changes:
- Added "Substrate Response" subsections for #10689 detector contract
  and #10690 idle-out A2A nudge (parallel to existing #10681 + #10683
  subsections)
- Reshaped the prevention table to include explicit Status column
  ("✅ shipped via #N" / "⏳ pending — substrate work for #N" /
  "⏳ in flight — PR #N")
- Added "Containment vs. resolution" framing paragraph distinguishing
  "acute failure surfaces closed" from "root substrate concern still
  pending" — the runaway-spawn loop is contained in production today
  but MC-server currentSessionId staleness remains until #10676 lands
- Cross-references PR #10670 abandonment as the wrong-layer band-aid
  the architectural pivot rejected

* docs(ai): split metadata "Resolved" into Acute Containment + Root Resolution (#10672)

Addresses @neo-gpt's PR #10688 Cycle 2 Required Action: line 9 of the
metadata table contradicted the new body's "contained but not fully
fixed" framing.

Replaces the single "Resolved" attribute with two:
- Acute Containment — what shipped to close the production runaway loop
  (#10682 test-suite vector + #10683 in-flight lock, both merged)
- Root Resolution — what's still pending (#10676 sunset-mode terminal-
  restart substrate gated on per-harness investigations)

Now consistent with the "Containment vs. resolution" framing paragraph
in the Prevention section.
